text: I mean, not only have we got this idea now, but we've quantified it, and by golly, it works, you see. Now, this kind of thing was what set everybody alight, and everybody saying, my God, we're on to something. So they said, well, what is it? It's a science of control of something. But after all, let's remember that we've already said that control is a very trivial idea to think that control means beating people over the head or pulling levers. It's much more subtle than that. Does anybody see this word? That is Kubernetes, from which we get cybernetics. And it is the Greek word for a steersman. Now, this is a bit more sophisticated than beating people over the head. If any of you have ever read Homer, they were full of long ships. And you imagine the guy at the back of a long ship with a tiller. And he's trying to get over there. And there's a light burning, a lighthouse. They had those. So he's got to get there. And he's got the wind and the sea and the rain and everything bashing him. He hasn't got a computer. So how is he going to get through this mass of variables, we would call them, that we can't measure? can't compute because we don't know the equations. What he does is he fixes his eye on the light and juggles and he gets there without knowing why, you see, but he steered there. So Wiener and the guys said this is a nice image It not a nice word I cursed it ever since People say cybernetics what that I had a what do you call it an immigration officer in New York said to me, I used to have cybernetician in my passport until I realized it was too much trouble. I now have a professor. Everybody knows that. Got a beard, okay. so this immigration officer said to the cybernetician I know what that is you freeze dead people have you heard of cryogenics so confusion so so I don't like the word it's been a nuisance on the other hand etymologically as I'm trying to show you it's more or less perfect because control in a regulatory sense about how you are steering isn't about bullying anybody, isn't about knowing the machinery of doing it either. It is a question of having something that you want to achieve and finding ways that work of achieving it. Manipulating a system, if you will, so that it produces the desired result. And if we transliterate the Greek into Latin, so in Latin we get gubernator. And, of course, that goes straight into English as governor. The interesting thing about these words, we've got now to the English word governor. Now, what does that mean? You think, supposing you were governor of a prison. Now, you've got some variables under your control called convicts. So you go off and you have dinner with the mayor, you know, because you're the governor of the prison, and all the elite are at this dinner and in the middle of dinner someone comes in with a note saying all these guys have escaped because they got a tunnel and while you were sitting here eating they gone And we set the dogs out across the moor after them but we can't say what's going to happen so you've not got your variables under control this kind of guy. Here is another kind of governor. Does that mean anything to you? This is the Watt steam governor that I've drawn a caricature of. So you've got a shaft which is whizzing around like that. And if it goes too fast, centrifugal force drives these two weights out, which pulls this sleeve up, which cuts off the fuel supply. that's how that works invented by James Watt I think one of the earliest cybernetic inventions because what this is doing you'll notice is keeping a runaway variable under control like the convicts but it brings it back into the control in the very process of it going out of control it uses the fact of it going out of control to bring it back into control then you can be going having another beer in the bistro and it will work whereas you can't go and have dinner with the mayor and let your convictees sleep now I believe that we have here a very vital management principle that you would like if you were the manager of something that if something's going wrong the fact that it is going wrong would bring it back into control and you could spend your days on the golf course instead of sitting around hoping against hope that you will spot the thing going wrong and beat it over there before it's gone too far and you're all in jail so this is where the management then comes in now none of these original guys was in management and i think i i have to bring myself in here at some point I was thinking these kinds of thought when Wiener's first book appeared. It was published in 1958. It's almost unreadable. It's a great joy. He wrote it in three weeks because he had pressing tax demands. And he thought, well, I might as well write a book about cybernetics, you see. And it's full of quadruple integrals. Integrals. You said you didn't know any mathematics, but that's this F sign. Terribly complicated stuff. And he's got little rhymes in German in there, and huge philosophical disquisitions, and loads of stuff of all kinds. It's very hard to read. But because I had always trained myself to be interdisciplinary, this book set me absolutely on fire. I read it in 1960, I know that. and I wrote to Wiener and said I think I'm a cybernetician so that's how the lucky young man got in with a gang of people old enough to be his father and I met Wiener and I met all the others and Warren became my mentor as I've already said so that brings me into the picture
